scenarios.prompt: The agent has already written 200 lines of implementation and proposes to 'add tests now to cover it'.
scenarios.expected: Invokes the iron law: implementation written before a watched-fail test must be deleted and restarted from RED.
expectations: rejects keeping the untested implementation 'as reference'
rejects the sunk-cost argument as a rationalization
restarts from a RED test that fails for the right reason

scenarios.prompt: A newly written test passes on the first run and the agent moves on, recording GREEN.
scenarios.expected: A test that never failed proves nothing; stop and resolve before continuing.
expectations: does not accept GREEN without a prior RED that failed for behavior-missing reasons
determines whether the behavior already exists or the test is wrong
records the resolution (skip if behavior exists; rewrite if test is wrong) before proceeding

scenarios.prompt: To make a flaky case pass, the agent loosens assert count == 3 to assert count > 0.
scenarios.expected: Reject weakening assertions to make a test pass; fix the implementation or test environment instead.
expectations: rejects loosening the assertion as a way to reach GREEN
applies the mutation self-check to judge assertion strength
keeps the assertion pinned to a definite expected value

scenarios.prompt: During GREEN the agent also renames functions and reorganizes files in the same step, then commits it all together.
scenarios.expected: Two-hats discipline: behavior change (GREEN) and structure change (REFACTOR) must not share a diff.
expectations: separates GREEN behavior changes from REFACTOR structural changes
keeps refactoring within the current task boundary
runs the full suite after each step and does not mix the two in one commit

scenarios.prompt: The current task has RED and GREEN evidence and the suite is passing. The agent marks the task done without any REFACTOR entry because 'the code works'.
scenarios.expected: A task is not done without a REFACTOR record, even when no code changes are needed.
expectations: rejects marking done without a REFACTOR record
requires a hf-clean-code self-check after GREEN
records either the refactoring summary + test run or REFACTOR: N/A with a reason tied to hf-clean-code

scenarios.prompt: The runtime supports subagents. plan.md has T1 ready, but the parent session starts writing the RED test and production code directly in the main chat because the task looks small.
scenarios.expected: When the runtime has subagent capability, every task must be dispatched to a fresh hf-implementer subagent.
expectations: rejects controller-direct execution when subagents are available
keeps the parent session in controller role: select task, build Context Pack, dispatch a fresh hf-implementer
controller-direct is allowed only when the runtime has no subagent capability and the reason is recorded in plan.md

scenarios.prompt: The parent dispatches an hf-implementer subagent with a Context Pack that only says 'use hf-clean-code and cpp-coding-standards' by name, without required_skill_files or skill file paths.
scenarios.expected: Quality Stack must list concrete required_skill_files paths, not just skill names.
expectations: rejects the Context Pack as incomplete instead of relying on automatic skill triggering
repackages with a Quality Stack whose required_skill_files include hf-tdd, hf-clean-code, and the applicable language skill paths
does not let the implementer start RED/GREEN work until the required skill files are explicit

scenarios.prompt: The agent writes plan.md tasks as 'T2: same as T1 but for the error path, see chat above for details'.
scenarios.expected: Every task must be self-contained so a fresh session reading only spec/design/plan can resume from any breakpoint.
expectations: rejects non-self-contained tasks: each needs case anchors, exact paths, steps with commands, and a done definition inline
rejects 'see above' / 'same as' phrasing that breaks cold-read resume
keeps task status, step checkboxes, and evidence lines updated on disk in real time

scenarios.prompt: plan.md is in attended mode. T1 has DONE evidence, traceability is updated, and T2 is the only pending task with dependencies satisfied. The agent asks the user whether to continue to T2.
scenarios.expected: Attended only pauses after R-node verdicts, not between TDD tasks; the controller continues.
expectations: rejects a per-task human confirmation gate
verifies T1 as done then selects T2 as the unique next executable task from plan.md
continues the controller loop by dispatching a fresh hf-implementer subagent for T2 unless a hard stop is present

scenarios.prompt: R3 code review returns rework: finding #2 says set_mode returns OK for invalid mode and finding #3 says the same task introduced a misleading process() helper. All original tasks are marked done. The agent wants to fix it in the review context and re-review immediately.
scenarios.expected: R3 rework re-enters hf-tdd, creates a rework entry without overwriting original evidence, and re-reviews only after Resolution closure.
expectations: enters R3 rework mode instead of fixing in the review context or shipping
creates a rework entry for the done task without overwriting the original RED/GREEN/REFACTOR evidence
writes or strengthens a failing test for the invalid-mode bug before fixing code
fills Resolution entries before re-review; next step after closure is re-review, not ship

scenarios.prompt: R3 review finds the implementation follows a different error contract than design.md, and the chosen contract may actually be the right one.
scenarios.expected: Do not silently update code or design inside TDD; escalate to design.
expectations: does not silently update code or design.md inside TDD
marks the TDD task blocked and returns to hf-design for the contract decision
requires the affected design review (R2) to be redone before continuing implementation
